title: artemisia campestris subsp. variabilis
intro: Artemisia campestris subsp. variabilis, commonly known as mugwort, is a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of mugwort include its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of PMS and menopause. Mugwort is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and smokes, and can also be used in traditional cooking and as a natural insect repellent.
